text:
n.
The are located, according to the Mnrkandeya-purdna,
between the Bahlikas and Abhiras. According to Pargiter, Vata-
dhana was a country on the eastern side of the Sutlej, southwards
from Ferozepur. Dey identifies it with Bhatnair. In the Mahabha-
rata (II. 32.8) mention is made of a Vatadhana settlement near
Madhyamika, modern Nagari, in Rajputana.
Takfasiln comprised the villages of Shahdheri, Sir-kap, Sir-
sukh and Kacheha-kot at a short distance to the north-west of
Rawalpindi.
Puskalavaia has been identified with Charsadda, about 17
miles north-east of Peshawar.
Ambara is explained by Utpala as Ambaravata.

Division of Globe XIV 181
Mnlava Fleet observes, 'Varahamihira places them too
much to the north, as they are undoubtedly the people of Malwa,
from whom ...the Vikrama era derived its origin.' It is possible
that our author refers here to a northern settlement of the Mala-
vas who lived between the lower Ravi and the Chenab in the 4th
century B.C.
